title: GitHub - Rentheria/llm-budget-cap: Atomic Redis spend cap for LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, …) — one Lua-atomic INCR+PEXPIRE so a bug or abuse can't blow up your bill.
excerpt: Atomic Redis spend cap for LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, …) — one Lua-atomic INCR+PEXPIRE so a bug or abuse can't blow up your bill. - Rentheria/llm-budget-cap
content_html:
Español

Atomic Redis spend cap for LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, …). An INCR + PEXPIRE counter that runs entirely inside a single Lua script, so a bug or abuse can't quietly bleed money out of your AI API bill.

  • ⚛️ Truly atomic. Increment + TTL-arm in one Lua execution: two near-simultaneous requests near the limit cannot both slip through. A GET + SET can (explained below).
  • 🎯 Small and focused. One class, one method. Zero third-party deps (just the ioredis client you already have).
  • 🕒 Configurable window. 24h by default; any window in milliseconds.
  • 🔑 One global counter or one per key (per tenant, per user, per IP).
  • 🛟 Fail-open by default. If Redis goes down, the operation proceeds (degraded) instead of taking your feature down — configurable.

Why it exists (the honest story)

We built this because we needed to cap an AI chatbot's spend without a bug letting it bleed money. You wire an assistant on top of Gemini/OpenAI, everything's fine in testing, and one day a loop, a scraper, or a distributed client with a thousand IPs spikes your bill to thousands of dollars while you sleep. Per-IP throttling doesn't save you from an attacker spread across many IPs.

The fix we ended up shipping three times in production was always the same: a Redis counter that counts calls within a window (24h) and cuts off once the limit is passed. The subtle part — the one that gives the guarantee — is that the INCR and the PEXPIRE have to run atomically, or two requests arriving at the same time right at the limit both slip through. This package is exactly that pattern, extracted and generalized so you don't have to rewrite it.

It's not a general-purpose rate limiter (for that, there's @nestjs/throttler, rate-limiter-flexible, etc.). It's a spend cap: a hard, cheap safety net against surprise bills.

Install

npm install llm-budget-cap ioredis

ioredis is a peer dependency (bring your own Redis client). Works with Express, Fastify, NestJS, or anything else — no framework dependency.

Minimal usage

import Redis from 'ioredis';
import { BudgetCap } from 'llm-budget-cap';

const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL);

// At most 500 LLM calls every 24h (default window).
const cap = new BudgetCap({ redis, key: 'gemini:daily', limit: 500 });

// Before calling the AI API:
const decision = await cap.checkAndIncrement();
if (!decision.allowed) {
  return res.status(429).json({ error: 'Daily AI budget exhausted.' });
}

const answer = await callGemini(userMessage); // your paid call, now protected

A cap per user / tenant / IP

const cap = new BudgetCap({
  redis,
  key: 'gemini:user', // combined as `gemini:user:<subKey>`
  limit: 50,
  windowMs: 60 * 60 * 1000, // 1 hour
});

const decision = await cap.checkAndIncrement(userId);
if (!decision.allowed) {
  // this particular user already spent their hourly quota
}

Why Lua-atomic instead of GET + SET

The naive approach has a race condition:

// ❌ UNSAFE: there is a window between the GET and the SET
const count = Number(await redis.get(key)) || 0;
if (count >= limit) return blocked();
await redis.set(key, count + 1); // ⬅️ another request may have read the SAME count

With the limit at 500 and the counter at 499, if two requests arrive almost at the same time:

  1. Request A does GET → reads 499. Decides: 499 < 500, proceeds.
  2. Request B does GET → reads 499 (A hasn't written yet). Decides: 499 < 500, proceeds.
  3. A writes 500. B writes 500.

Both got through. The counter ended up at 500, but 501 paid calls were made. Under high traffic near the limit, this isn't theoretical — it happens.

Redis's INCR is atomic, but the real problem is coordinating the INCR with setting the TTL (PEXPIRE). If you do it in two commands:

// ❌ A different race: if the process dies between these two lines,
// the key is left with NO expiration forever (a leak + the cap never resets).
const count = await redis.incr(key);
if (count === 1) await redis.expire(key, ttl);

The fix is to put everything inside a single Lua script, which Redis runs start to finish without interleaving other commands:

local current = redis.call("INCR", KEYS[1])
local ttl = redis.call("PTTL", KEYS[1])
if current == 1 or ttl < 0 then
  redis.call("PEXPIRE", KEYS[1], ARGV[1])
end
return current

This way, two simultaneous requests cannot read the same value: every checkAndIncrement gets a unique, increasing current, and the TTL is always armed on the window's first call. It's the only way for the cap to be a guarantee, not a suggestion. (The script uses PTTL/PEXPIRE instead of PEXPIRE ... NX, so it also works on Redis < 7.)

The exact script is exported as ATOMIC_BUDGET_CAP_LUA in case you want to audit or reuse it.

API

new BudgetCap(options)

Option Type Default Description
redis RedisEvalClient — ioredis-compatible client (eval(script, numKeys, ...args)).
key string — Base counter key. With a subKey it's combined as key:subKey.
limit number — Max operations allowed per window (integer ≥ 1).
windowMs number 86_400_000 (24h) Window length in ms, counted from the first hit.
failOpen boolean true If Redis fails, let the operation through (degraded) or throw the error?

checkAndIncrement(subKey?) → Promise<BudgetCapDecision>

Atomically increments the counter and returns the decision. The increment happens before the decision: a blocked call still counts within the window, but blocked calls do not extend the TTL (the window is fixed from the first hit, not sliding).

interface BudgetCapDecision {
  allowed: boolean; // true if you can proceed (count <= limit)
  count: number; // counter value AFTER this increment (0 if degraded)
  limit: number; // the configured limit
  remaining: number; // operations left: max(0, limit - count)
  degraded: boolean; // true if Redis failed and this is a fail-open fallback
}

Fail-open vs fail-closed

By default (failOpen: true), if Redis is unreachable checkAndIncrement returns { allowed: true, degraded: true } instead of throwing: we'd rather have a paid feature go briefly unmetered during a Redis outage than take the whole feature down. Check decision.degraded if you want to log/alert on it. With failOpen: false the Redis error is re-thrown and you decide.

node-redis instead of ioredis

The library only needs an eval(script, numKeys, ...args) method. node-redis (v4)'s eval has a different signature; wrap it:

const adapter = {
  eval: (script, numKeys, ...args) =>
    client.eval(script, {
      keys: args.slice(0, numKeys),
      arguments: args.slice(numKeys).map(String),
    }),
};
const cap = new BudgetCap({ redis: adapter, key: 'gemini:daily', limit: 500 });

Development

npm install
npm run lint        # ESLint (typescript-eslint strict, type-checked)
npm run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
npm test            # Vitest, single worker; atomicity tests use real Redis
npm run build       # tsup → ESM + CJS + types

The atomicity tests need a real Redis (REDIS_URL, default redis://127.0.0.1:6399):

docker run --rm -p 6399:6379 redis:7-alpine

License

MIT © Alejandro Rentheria

sentences.en: checkAndIncrement(subKey?) → Promise<BudgetCapDecision>

blocks.html_en: Atomically increments the counter and returns the decision. The increment happens before the decision: a blocked call still counts within the window, but blocked calls do not extend the TTL (the window is fixed from the first hit, not sliding).

sentences.en: Atomically increments the counter and returns the decision.
sentences.zh: 以原子方式递增计数器并返回决策。

sentences.en: The increment happens before the decision: a blocked call still counts within the window, but blocked calls do not extend the TTL (the window is fixed from the first hit, not sliding).
sentences.zh: 增量发生在决策之前：阻塞的调用仍然在窗口内计数，但阻塞的调用不会延长 TTL（窗口从第一次命中开始是固定的，而不是滑动）。

sentences.en: Fail-open vs fail-closed
sentences.zh: 故障开放与故障关闭

blocks.html_en: By default (failOpen: true), if Redis is unreachable checkAndIncrement returns { allowed: true, degraded: true } instead of throwing: we'd rather have a paid feature go briefly unmetered during a Redis outage than take the whole feature down. Check decision.degraded if you want to log/alert on it. With failOpen: false the Redis error is re-thrown and you decide.

sentences.en: By default (failOpen: true), if Redis is unreachable checkAndIncrement returns { allowed: true, degraded: true } instead of throwing: we'd rather have a paid feature go briefly unmetered during a Redis outage than take the whole feature down.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下（failOpen: true），如果 Redis 无法访问， checkAndIncrement 返回 { allowed: true, degraded: true } 而不是抛出：我们宁愿让付费功能在 Redis 中断期间暂时无法计量，也不愿关闭整个功能。

sentences.en: Check decision.degraded if you want to log/alert on it.
sentences.zh: 如果您想记录/警报，请检查 Decision.degraded。

sentences.en: With failOpen: false the Redis error is re-thrown and you decide.
sentences.zh: 使用failOpen: false，Redis 错误将被重新抛出，由您决定。

content_html_zh:
西班牙语

LLM API（OpenAI、Gemini、Anthropic 等）的 Atomic Redis 支出上限。 INCR + PEXPIRE 计数器完全在单个 Lua 脚本内运行，因此错误或滥用不会悄悄地从您的 AI API 账单中流失金钱。

  • ⚛️ 真正的原子。 一次 Lua 执行中的增量 + TTL-arm：接近极限的两个几乎同时的请求不能同时通过。 GET + SET 即可（下面解释）。
  • 🎯 小而专注。 一个类，一种方法。 零第三方依赖（只有您已有的 ioredis 客户端）。
  • 🕒 可配置的窗口。 默认24小时；任何窗口（以毫秒为单位）。
  • 🔑 一个全局计数器或每个键一个（每个租户、每个用户、每个 IP）。
  • 🛟 默认失败打开。 如果 Redis 宕机，操作会继续（降级），而不是关闭您的功能 — 可配置。

为什么它存在（诚实的故事）

我们之所以构建这个，是因为我们需要限制人工智能聊天机器人的支出，同时避免出现让它流失金钱的错误。 你在 Gemini/OpenAI 上连接了一个助手，测试中一切正常，有一天，一个循环、一个爬虫或一个具有一千个 IP 的分布式客户端会让你在睡觉时的账单飙升至数千美元。 按 IP 限制并不能帮助您免受分布在多个 IP 上的攻击者的侵害。

我们最终在生产中发布了三次的修复程序始终是相同的：Redis 计数器对窗口（24 小时）内的调用进行计数，并在超过限制后停止。 微妙的部分（提供保证的部分）是 INCR 和 PEXPIRE 必须以原子方式运行，否则两个同时到达限制的请求都会漏掉。 这个包正是这种模式，经过提取和概括，因此您不必重写它。

它不是通用的速率限制器（为此，有@nestjs/throttler、灵活的速率限制器等）。 这是一个支出上限：一个针对意外账单的坚硬、廉价的安全网。

安装

npm install llm-budget-cap ioredis

ioredis 是对等依赖项（自带 Redis 客户端）。 可与 Express、Fastify、NestJS 或其他任何工具配合使用 — 无框架依赖性。

最少使用量

import Redis from 'ioredis';
import { BudgetCap } from 'llm-budget-cap';

const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL);

// At most 500 LLM calls every 24h (default window).
const cap = new BudgetCap({ redis, key: 'gemini:daily', limit: 500 });

// Before calling the AI API:
const decision = await cap.checkAndIncrement();
if (!decision.allowed) {
  return res.status(429).json({ error: 'Daily AI budget exhausted.' });
}

const answer = await callGemini(userMessage); // your paid call, now protected

每个用户/租户/IP 的上限

const cap = new BudgetCap({
  redis,
  key: 'gemini:user', // combined as `gemini:user:<subKey>`
  limit: 50,
  windowMs: 60 * 60 * 1000, // 1 hour
});

const decision = await cap.checkAndIncrement(userId);
if (!decision.allowed) {
  // this particular user already spent their hourly quota
}

为什么 Lua-atomic 而不是 GET + SET

天真的方法有一个竞争条件：

// ❌ UNSAFE: there is a window between the GET and the SET
const count = Number(await redis.get(key)) || 0;
if (count >= limit) return blocked();
await redis.set(key, count + 1); // ⬅️ another request may have read the SAME count

限制为 500，计数器为 499，如果两个请求几乎同时到达：

  1. 请求 A 执行 GET → 读取 499。 决定：499 < 500，继续。
  2. 请求 B 执行 GET → 读取 499（A 尚未写入）。 决定：499 < 500，继续。
  3. A写了500。 B 写了 500。

两人都通过了。 柜台最终显示为 500，但拨打了 501 个付费电话。 在接近极限的高流量下，这不是理论上的——它确实发生了。

Redis 的 INCR 是原子的，但真正的问题是协调 INCR 与设置 TTL (PEXPIRE)。 如果您用两个命令执行此操作：

// ❌ A different race: if the process dies between these two lines,
// the key is left with NO expiration forever (a leak + the cap never resets).
const count = await redis.incr(key);
if (count === 1) await redis.expire(key, ttl);

解决方法是将所有内容放入单个 Lua 脚本中，Redis 从头到尾运行该脚本，而不会交错其他命令：

local current = redis.call("INCR", KEYS[1])
local ttl = redis.call("PTTL", KEYS[1])
if current == 1 or ttl < 0 then
  redis.call("PEXPIRE", KEYS[1], ARGV[1])
end
return current

这样，两个同时请求就无法读取相同的值：每个 checkAndIncrement 都会获得一个唯一的、递增的电流，并且 TTL 始终在窗口的第一次调用时准备就绪。 这是上限成为保证而不是建议的唯一方法。 （该脚本使用 PTTL/PEXPIRE 而不是 PEXPIRE ... NX，因此它也适用于 Redis < 7。）

确切的脚本将导出为 ATOMIC_BUDGET_CAP_LUA，以防您想要审核或重用它。

API

新的预算上限（选项）

选项 Type 默认 描述
redis Redis评估客户端 — ioredis 兼容客户端 (eval(script, numKeys, ...args))。
key 细绳 — 基本计数器键。 使用 subKey 时，它会组合为 key:subKey。
limit 数字 — 每个窗口允许的最大操作数（整数 ≥ 1）。
窗口女士 数字 86_400_000 (24h) 窗口长度（以毫秒为单位），从第一次命中开始计算。
打开失败 布尔值 true 如果 Redis 失败，让操作通过（降级）还是抛出错误？

checkAndIncrement(subKey?) → Promise<BudgetCapDecision>

以原子方式递增计数器并返回决策。 增量发生在决策之前：阻塞的调用仍然在窗口内计数，但阻塞的调用不会延长 TTL（窗口从第一次命中开始是固定的，而不是滑动）。

interface BudgetCapDecision {
  allowed: boolean; // true if you can proceed (count <= limit)
  count: number; // counter value AFTER this increment (0 if degraded)
  limit: number; // the configured limit
  remaining: number; // operations left: max(0, limit - count)
  degraded: boolean; // true if Redis failed and this is a fail-open fallback
}

故障开放与故障关闭

默认情况下（failOpen: true），如果 Redis 无法访问， checkAndIncrement 返回 { allowed: true, degraded: true } 而不是抛出：我们宁愿让付费功能在 Redis 中断期间暂时无法计量，也不愿关闭整个功能。 如果您想记录/警报，请检查 Decision.degraded。 使用failOpen: false，Redis 错误将被重新抛出，由您决定。

节点-redis代替ioredis

该库只需要一个 eval(script, numKeys, ...args) 方法。 node-redis (v4) 的 eval 有不同的签名；包裹它：

const adapter = {
  eval: (script, numKeys, ...args) =>
    client.eval(script, {
      keys: args.slice(0, numKeys),
      arguments: args.slice(numKeys).map(String),
    }),
};
const cap = new BudgetCap({ redis: adapter, key: 'gemini:daily', limit: 500 });

发展

npm install
npm run lint        # ESLint (typescript-eslint strict, type-checked)
npm run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
npm test            # Vitest, single worker; atomicity tests use real Redis
npm run build       # tsup → ESM + CJS + types

原子性测试需要一个真实的Redis（REDIS_URL，默认redis://127.0.0.1:6399）：

docker run --rm -p 6399:6379 redis:7-alpine

执照

什么 © Alejandro Rentheria
